steps.title: Identify the midsegment
steps.content: D is the midpoint of AB and E is the midpoint of BC, so DE is the midsegment of triangle ABC connecting midpoints of two sides. By the Triangle Midsegment Theorem, DE is parallel to AC and $DE = \frac{1}{2} AC$.

steps.title: Solve for AC and test all choices
steps.content:
$$AC = 2 \times DE = 2 \times 14 = 28$$
  • Choice A — 28: $2 \times 14 = 28$. ✓
  • Choice B — 26: Does not equal $2 \times 14$. ✗
  • Choice C — 22: Does not equal $2 \times 14$. ✗
  • Choice D — 16: Equals $BD + DE = 8 + 14 - 6$? No valid derivation gives 16. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
